refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-003 Refined: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and tele-operators to achieve a measured energy efficiency of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target (customer-defined threshold) over the agreed evaluation period, through integrated monitoring, control optimization, and real-time feedback mechanisms.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit linkage to the mechanisms (monitoring, control, feedback) that enable efficiency achievement. Refinement clarifies that energy efficiency is not a passive property but an active capability requiring measurement, visibility, and operator decision support. This aligns with the high safety criticality constraint by ensuring operators have visibility into energy consumption patterns.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure energy_per_ton = total_energy_used / total_ton_moved over the agreed evaluation period (e.g., 30-day operational window). Verify that measured value ≤ E_target. Collect telemetry from Vehicle power systems and cross-validate with site-level energy metering. Confirm operator feedback mechanisms are functional and used.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh or equivalent) from all propulsion, hydraulic, and auxiliary systems.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log work output (ton_moved or equivalent productivity metric) from task execution.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall compute and display real-time energy_per_ton ratio to operators and tele-operators.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall provide control recommendations or automated optimization to reduce energy_per_ton below E_target.
CR-NGHE-003.5: The NGHE solution shall alert Safety Manager and Maintenance if energy_per_ton trends indicate anomalies or equipment degradation.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Passive energy monitoring only (measure and log, no optimization or feedback)
DP-B: Active energy management system with real-time telemetry, operator feedback dashboard, and rule-based control optimization
DP-C: Predictive energy optimization using machine learning models trained on historical worksite data to recommend operator actions
DP-D: Hybrid approach combining real-time telemetry (DP-B) with predictive optimization (DP-C) and safety-gated control authority
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Hybrid real-time telemetry + predictive optimization with safety-gated control authority. Real-time energy_per_ton visibility is provided to operators via Telemetry interface; predictive models (trained offline on historical data) generate optimization recommendations sent via Tasking interface; safety-critical control changes are gated by Safety Services module to ensure no unsafe state transitions.

potential_sources: Coupling between Vehicle power measurement (DP-A sub-component) and Teleoperation control authority (DP-D sub-component) is managed via explicit Tasking interface handoff.
Coupling between Safety Services gating logic and predictive optimization recommendations is managed via SafetyAlert interface.
Coupling between real-time telemetry collection and historical data aggregation for model training is managed via asynchronous Telemetry logging.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D avoids tight coupling by using message-based interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) rather than direct function calls. Predictive model training is decoupled from real-time control via offline batch processing. Safety gating is a clear, testable constraint on control authority diffusion.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Energy consumption can be accurately measured from Vehicle power systems (motor current, hydraulic pressure, fuel flow) with <5% error.
ASSUMPTION: Work output (ton_moved) can be reliably inferred from Vehicle sensors (load cell, position, time) or external site metering.
ASSUMPTION: Predictive models can be trained on historical worksite data with sufficient accuracy (R² > 0.8) to generate actionable recommendations.
RISK: If energy measurement is noisy or biased, real-time feedback to operators may be misleading, reducing trust and adoption.
RISK: If predictive model is poorly trained or overfits to one worksite, recommendations may not generalize to new sites, reducing effectiveness.
RISK: Safety gating logic may introduce latency in control response, potentially reducing operator responsiveness in time-critical scenarios.
RISK: High safety criticality requires rigorous validation of predictive model outputs before they influence control authority; this adds development and certification cost.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Recommend proceeding with DP-D but with explicit risk mitigation: (1) Conduct sensor validation study to confirm energy measurement accuracy; (2) Establish model validation protocol with independent test data from multiple worksites; (3) Define safety gating rules explicitly and subject to formal verification; (4) Implement operator override capability with audit logging to ensure human-in-the-loop control.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration module responsible for orchestrating energy efficiency capability across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-modules. Owns the energy_per_ton computation logic, real-time dashboard, and optimization recommendation engine. Interfaces with all sub-modules via Telemetry (inbound sensor data), Tasking (outbound control recommendations), SafetyAlert (bidirectional safety gating), and Network Link (external data ingestion for model training).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Inbound stream of energy consumption (kWh), work output (ton_moved), and Vehicle state from Vehicle module. Used to compute real-time energy_per_ton and detect anomalies.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Outbound control recommendations (e.g., 'reduce hydraulic pressure by 5%', 'optimize bucket fill cycle') sent to Teleoperation module for operator approval or automated execution (if safety-gated).

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional safety gating: NGHE Solution sends optimization recommendations to Safety Services for validation; Safety Services returns approval/rejection and alerts if energy_per_ton anomalies indicate equipment degradation or unsafe conditions.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Inbound connection for ingesting historical worksite data, weather conditions, and external benchmarks used to train and update predictive models offline.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of energy efficiency orchestration. All sub-components (measurement, computation, recommendation, gating) logically belong here. Minimal diffusion: only Telemetry inbound and Tasking outbound cross module boundaries, which is appropriate for a system-level capability.

Vehicle module owns energy measurement sensors and propulsion control, but does not own the energy_per_ton computation logic, optimization recommendations, or safety gating. If energy efficiency logic were embedded in Vehicle, it would create tight coupling between Vehicle control and system-level optimization, violating separation of concerns. Vehicle should remain a sensor/actuator provider.

Teleoperation module owns operator interface and control authority, but energy efficiency optimization is a system-level concern, not a teleoperation-specific concern. Embedding optimization logic in Teleoperation would make it difficult to apply the same optimization to autonomous or semi-autonomous modes. Teleoperation should remain a control interface provider.

Safety Services owns safety gating and anomaly alerting, which are critical sub-functions of energy efficiency. However, Safety Services should not own the predictive model or optimization recommendation engine, as those are not inherently safety-critical (they are safety-constrained). Ownership should be split: NGHE Solution owns optimization logic, Safety Services owns gating and alerting.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy efficiency capability is a system-level cross-cutting concern that requires orchestration of measurement (Vehicle), control (Teleoperation), and safety gating (Safety Services). The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner because it sits at the system integration layer and can coordinate these sub-modules via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). This avoids diffusion of optimization logic across multiple modules and maintains clear separation of concerns.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution becomes a 'god module' that owns too many cross-cutting concerns, it may become difficult to test and maintain. Mitigation: Define clear sub-module boundaries within NGHE Solution (e.g., TelemetryAggregator, EnergyCompute, OptimizationEngine, SafetyGate) with explicit internal interfaces.

RISK: If predictive model training is embedded in NGHE Solution, it may require large amounts of historical data and compute resources, creating a deployment bottleneck. Mitigation: Decouple model training (offline, cloud-based) from model inference (real-time, edge-based) via Network Link interface.

RISK: If Teleoperation module does not have visibility into optimization recommendations, operators may not understand why control authority is being constrained, reducing trust. Mitigation: Ensure Tasking interface includes human-readable explanation of recommendations (e.g., 'Reduce pressure to save 5% energy').
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Proceed with NGHE Solution as owner, but explicitly define internal sub-module structure (TelemetryAggregator, EnergyCompute, OptimizationEngine, SafetyGate) with clear ownership and interface contracts. Ensure Network Link is used only for offline model training, not real-time control, to avoid latency and reliability issues.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (hybrid real-time telemetry + predictive optimization with safety-gated control) directly addresses the refined FR by providing continuous measurement (CR-NGHE-003.1, 003.2), real-time feedback (003.3), optimization recommendations (003.4), and safety alerting (003.5). The DP is neither over-specified (it does not mandate a particular algorithm or hardware) nor under-specified (it clearly defines the functional flow: measure → compute → recommend → gate → execute). Coupling is managed via explicit interfaces.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK: If optimization recommendation logic is not centralized in NGHE Solution, it may be duplicated in Teleoperation (for operator-facing recommendations) and Safety Services (for safety-gated recommendations), creating inconsistency and maintenance burden. Mitigation: Enforce single source of truth for optimization engine in NGHE Solution; Teleoperation and Safety Services consume recommendations via Tasking and SafetyAlert interfaces.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution containment score of 0.95 indicates strong architectural fit. The four external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are all defined in the known interface set, confirming that the IM boundary does not require inventing new interfaces. Internal sub-module structure (TelemetryAggregator, EnergyCompute, OptimizationEngine, SafetyGate) is not yet detailed but is feasible within NGHE Solution scope.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-003 is feasible via measurement of energy_per_ton over the agreed evaluation period. NGHE Solution module owns the computation logic and can provide audit logs of energy consumption, work output, and energy_per_ton ratio. Verification does not require access to internal Vehicle or Teleoperation implementation details, only the Telemetry interface outputs.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of sub-FRs to modules is clear: CR-NGHE-003.1 (energy measurement) is allocated to Vehicle with NGHE Solution as consumer; CR-NGHE-003.2 (work output measurement) is allocated to Vehicle with NGHE Solution as consumer; CR-NGHE-003.3 (real-time display) is allocated to NGHE Solution with Teleoperation as consumer; CR-NGHE-003.4 (optimization recommendations) is allocated to NGHE Solution with Teleoperation and Safety Services as consumers; CR-NGHE-003.5 (anomaly alerting) is allocated to NGHE Solution with Safety Services as consumer.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define the internal sub-module structure of NGHE Solution (TelemetryAggregator, EnergyCompute, OptimizationEngine, SafetyGate) in the next decomposition level to reduce diffusion risk.

Establish a formal interface contract for Tasking (optimization recommendations) that includes human-readable explanation, confidence level, and estimated energy savings, to support operator trust and decision-making.
Define the safety gating rules explicitly (e.g., 'reject any recommendation that increases peak power draw above 110% of rated capacity') and subject them to formal verification before deployment.
Conduct a sensor validation study to confirm that energy consumption and work output can be measured with sufficient accuracy (<5% error) to support reliable energy_per_ton computation.

decision.justification: The refined FR is well-formed and addresses the customer need for energy efficiency KPI support. The selected DP (hybrid real-time telemetry + predictive optimization with safety-gated control) is sound and avoids over-coupling. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) has strong containment fit (0.95) and uses only known interfaces. However, the DP and IM are not yet sufficiently detailed to proceed to implementation. The internal sub-module structure of NGHE Solution (TelemetryAggregator, EnergyCompute, OptimizationEngine, SafetyGate) must be decomposed to clarify ownership, interface contracts, and safety gating logic. Additionally, the high safety criticality constraint requires explicit verification of the safety gating rules before proceeding to detailed design. Therefore, recommend decomposing CR-NGHE-003 into its five sub-FRs and performing a second-level functional decomposition for each.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh or equivalent) from all propulsion, hydraulic, and auxiliary systems.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log work output (ton_moved or equivalent productivity metric) from task execution.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall compute and display real-time energy_per_ton ratio to operators and tele-operators.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall provide control recommendations or automated optimization to reduce energy_per_ton below E_target.
CR-NGHE-003.5: The NGHE solution shall alert Safety Manager and Maintenance if energy_per_ton trends indicate anomalies or equipment degradation.

assumptions: Energy consumption can be accurately measured from Vehicle power systems (motor current, hydraulic pressure, fuel flow) with <5% error.
Work output (ton_moved) can be reliably inferred from Vehicle sensors (load cell, position, time) or external site metering.
Predictive models can be trained on historical worksite data with sufficient accuracy (R² > 0.8) to generate actionable recommendations.
Safety gating rules can be formally specified and verified to ensure no unsafe state transitions.
Operators will trust and adopt optimization recommendations if they are transparent, explainable, and include confidence levels and estimated energy savings.
Network Link interface is available for offline model training and does not introduce real-time latency or reliability issues.
open_risks: RISK: Energy measurement noise or bias may reduce operator trust in real-time feedback. Mitigation: Conduct sensor validation study and implement noise filtering.
RISK: Predictive model may not generalize to new worksites or equipment variants. Mitigation: Establish model validation protocol with independent test data from multiple sites.
RISK: Safety gating logic may introduce latency in control response, reducing operator responsiveness. Mitigation: Define latency budget and implement real-time safety gating (not cloud-based).
RISK: High safety criticality requires rigorous validation of predictive model outputs. Mitigation: Implement formal verification of safety gating rules and conduct independent safety assessment.
RISK: Optimization recommendations may conflict with operator preferences or worksite-specific constraints. Mitigation: Implement operator override capability with audit logging.
evidence_needed: Sensor validation study confirming energy consumption and work output measurement accuracy (<5% error).
Predictive model validation report with R² > 0.8 on independent test data from multiple worksites.
Formal specification and verification of safety gating rules.
Operator feedback study on transparency, explainability, and trust in optimization recommendations.
Latency analysis of real-time safety gating logic to confirm it meets operator responsiveness requirements.
Independent safety assessment confirming that energy efficiency optimization does not introduce new hazards or reduce safety margins.
